description: The 2021 Greenlandic election was dominated by the controversy surrounding the Kuannersuit (Kvanefjeld) mining project, which involved rare earth elements but also radioactive uranium. The pro-mining coalition was defeated by the Inuit Ataqatigiit party, effectively halting the project.

key_events.description: Public fears grow over environmental contamination in the fragile Arctic ecosystem.
The Inuit Ataqatigiit party pivots the election focus toward preserving traditional fishing and hunting lifestyles.
Election results confirm a mandate to ban uranium mining in Greenland.

calibration_notes: Demonstrates the tension between economic resource exploitation and cultural/environmental preservation.
